title: Generic commercial loan facility schema
description: Version 1.0.0 generic schema for a single facility-level commercial loan document. The schema deliberately separates a required normalised core from optional concern-specific blocks so that bilateral facilities, term sheets and facility amendments can share a comparable shape without losing legally material detail.

percentage.description: Percentage expressed as a percent, not a decimal fraction; for example 5.25 means 5.25%.

source_ref.description: Optional evidence pointer back to the source document.

reference.description: Clause, schedule, section or page reference, preserving document notation where possible.

title.description: Parenthetical or heading title, if present in the document.

quote.description: Short supporting quotation from the source.

role.description: Role as stated in the document, e.g. Borrower, Lender, Agent, Guarantor.

jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction of incorporation, organisation or establishment.

hierarchy_note.description: Explanation of why the document is treated as facility-level rather than deal-level or loan-instrument-level.

facility_id.description: Stable identifier for the facility within this document; use 'main' for a single unnamed facility.

subtype.description: More specific market label if the generic type is insufficient.

commitment_amount_text.description: Verbatim amount string where useful, e.g. where the amount is expressed in words.

courts.description: Court/forum selection, including exclusive/non-exclusive wording where available.

facility_optional_blocks.description: Concern-specific extensions. Omit blocks that are not present in the source document rather than inventing values. The normalised facility core stays deliberately small; detailed operational, legal and compliance concerns are optional because they are not uniformly present across facility agreements, term sheets and amendments.

reference_rate_name.description: Rate name as it should appear to downstream users. Prefer the specific benchmark where available, e.g. SONIA rather than a generic RFR label.

letters_of_credit_permitted.description: Populate only where the document clearly permits or excludes letters of credit for this facility.

collateral_perfection_summary.description: Narrative summary of perfection requirements where the source gives enough detail.

qualifier.description: Materiality, satisfaction or no-waiver qualifier that must not be dropped.

formula_summary.description: Plain-English summary of the covenant formula if a full calculation definition is not extracted.

full_text.description: Full covenant text where exhaustive legal-text capture is required.

exhaustive_undertakings_captured.description: True only if all undertakings in scope have been captured, not merely summarised.

omitted_undertaking_categories.description: Use when extraction intentionally excludes boilerplate or categories outside scope.

full_text.description: Full event-of-default text where exhaustive legal-text capture is required.

remedy_or_consequence.description: Acceleration, cancellation, enforcement or other consequence tied to this event, if stated.

exhaustive_events_captured.description: True only if all Events of Default in scope have been captured, not merely summarised.

acceleration_and_enforcement_rights.description: Full or summarised text describing lender rights after an Event of Default.

lender_statutory_authority.description: Preserve role distinctions such as statutory power versus authority where the document makes them.

amendment_overlay.description: Use for facility-amendment documents that modify an existing facility rather than creating a fresh facility from scratch.

source_trace.description: Extraction-level metadata. This block is not a substitute for field-level source_refs where precision matters.

source_text_path.description: Path to extracted readable text used for PDF-grounded extraction, usually produced by extract_pdf_text. Required by validation for pdf_direct/hybrid PDF source documents.

source_text_char_count.description: Character count of the extracted readable source text when available.

payments_and_settlement.description: Optional operational block for payment routing and settlement mechanics. It is optional because detailed bank-account instructions may be absent or redacted in some facility documents.

payment_waterfall.description: Order of application of payments if stated.

notice_mechanics.description: Optional legal/operational block for formal notices and communications mechanics.

transfers_and_assignments.description: Optional legal/trading block for lender transfer, assignment, novation and participation restrictions.

normalised_formula.description: Machine-readable or pseudo-code formula, if safely derivable from the document.

covenant_calculation_definitions.description: Optional credit/legal block for complete covenant formula extraction. Kept outside core because many simple bilateral facilities and term sheets have no financial covenant package.

exhaustive_calculations_captured.description: True only if all covenant formulas and defined terms in scope have been captured.

undertakings_and_events_full_text.description: Optional legal block for exhaustive undertaking and Event of Default text. Use when legal review needs complete clause text rather than summaries.

collateral_perfection.description: Optional security/legal operations block for collateral perfection actions. Use where the document specifies registration, filing, delivery, notice or post-closing perfection steps.

compliance_kyc_aml_sanctions.description: Optional compliance block for KYC, AML, sanctions and anti-corruption information. Generally extracted only where the facility document itself states the requirement or representation.

operational_servicing_calendar.description: Optional operations block converting document dates, date rules and recurring obligations into servicing events.

generated_from_fields.description: Schema paths used to generate the calendar event list, where applicable.
